message:
fix: input path detection false-positives on filenames with brackets

The `looks_like_inline_mermaid` function was returning true for any
input containing brackets or parentheses, causing files like
`report(final).mmd` or `data[backup].txt` to be treated as inline
mermaid content instead of being read from disk.

Fix: Add `has_node_definition_pattern` that checks for actual mermaid
node syntax (ID followed by bracket) and `looks_like_filename` that
detects file extension patterns. Filenames with extensions are now
correctly identified and read from disk.

Adds regression tests for both bracket and paren filename patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
